frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing FLS trainer with Penrose drain, needle drivers, and scissors visible
Right needle driver grasping needle with suture attached
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears loaded at appropriate 2/3 position

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through first mark on drain
Needle exiting through second mark on drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through both marks visible

frame_analyses.description: Needle released after complete passage through drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both marks appear well-approximated by suture

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot formation with right hand leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right hand preparing to create surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Completing surgeon's knot (double throw) with right hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double wrap visible, appropriate tension maintained

frame_analyses.description: Scissors visible, changing to left hand for second throw
Left hand completing single throw in opposite direction
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Single throw executed with left hand leading

frame_analyses.description: Final single throw with right hand leading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw completed with proper hand alternation

frame_analyses.description: Final knot secured, preparing to cut suture ends
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure with no gap in drain

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends cut with scissors, task completed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cuts of both suture tails inside trainer

penalties.description: Minor deviation at first mark, approximately 1mm
Minor deviation at second mark, approximately 1mm

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with appropriate tension

confidence_rationale: Clear view of all major phases and knot sequence. Some uncertainty in exact millimeter measurements due to camera angle, but overall performance well-documented.
cannot_determine: Exact precision of suture entry points due to viewing angle limitations
technique_summary: Excellent overall performance with expert-level timing and proper technique execution. All required knot sequence elements completed correctly with appropriate hand alternation.
strengths: Very fast completion time (55s vs 112s proficiency target)
Proper surgeon's knot followed by two single throws
Correct hand alternation between all throws
Clean suture placement with minimal deviation
No gap visible in drain closure
improvement_suggestions: Minor refinement in needle entry precision to minimize deviation from marks
